children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Under Secretary may establish MBDA Rural Business Centers.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: With respect to an MBDA Rural Business Center established by the Under Secretary, the Under Secretary shall establish the MBDA Rural Business Center in partnership with an eligible entity in accordance with paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: the eligible entity shall provide space, facilities, and staffing for the MBDA Rural Business Center;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Under Secretary shall provide funding for, and oversight with respect to, the MBDA Rural Business Center; and

children.label: iii
children.text: subject to subparagraph (B), the eligible entity shall match 20 percent of the amount of the funding provided by the Under Secretary under clause (ii), which may be calculated to include the costs of providing the space, facilities, and staffing under clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: the eligible entity shall match less than 20 percent of the amount of the funding provided by the Under Secretary under subparagraph (A)(ii); or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the Under Secretary makes a determination, upon a demonstration by the eligible entity of substantial need, the eligible entity shall not be required to provide any match with respect to the funding provided by the Under Secretary under subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: Eligible funds
children.label: C Eligible funds
children.text: An eligible entity may provide matching funds required under an MBDA Rural Business Center agreement with Federal funds received from other Federal programs.

children.heading: Term
children.label: 3 Term
children.text: The initial term of an MBDA Rural Business Center agreement shall be not less than 3 years.

children.heading: Extension
children.label: 4 Extension
children.text: The Under Secretary and an eligible entity may agree to extend the term of an MBDA Rural Business Center agreement with respect to an MBDA Rural Business Center.

children.label: A
children.text: rural minority business enterprises; or

children.label: B
children.text: minority business enterprises that are located more than 50 miles from an MBDA Business Center (other than that MBDA Rural Business Center);

children.label: i
children.text: the adoption of broadband internet access service (as defined in section 8.1(b) of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation), digital literacy skills, and e-commerce by rural minority business enterprises;

children.label: ii
children.text: advanced manufacturing;

children.label: iii
children.text: the promotion of manufacturing in the United States;

children.label: iv
children.text: ways in which rural minority business enterprises can meet gaps in the supply chain of critical supplies and essential goods and services for the United States;

children.label: v
children.text: improving the connectivity of rural minority business enterprises through transportation and logistics;

children.label: vi
children.text: promoting trade and export opportunities by rural minority business enterprises;

children.label: vii
children.text: securing financial capital;

children.label: viii
children.text: facilitating entrepreneurship in rural areas; and

children.label: ix
children.text: creating jobs in rural areas; and

children.label: B
children.text: any other issue relating to the unique challenges faced by rural minority business enterprises; and
provide education, training, and legal, financial, and technical assistance to minority business enterprises.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Under Secretary shall issue a Notice of Funding Opportunity requesting applications from eligible entities that desire to enter into MBDA Rural Business Center agreements.November 15, 2021

children.label: i
children.text: the ability to collaborate with governmental and private sector entities to leverage capabilities of minority business enterprises through public-private partnerships;

children.label: ii
children.text: the research and extension capacity to support minority business enterprises;

children.label: iii
children.text: knowledge of the community that the eligible entity serves and the ability to conduct effective outreach to that community to advance the goals of an MBDA Rural Business Center;

children.label: iv
children.text: the ability to provide innovative business solutions, including access to contracting opportunities, markets, and capital;

children.label: v
children.text: the ability to provide services that advance the development of science, technology, engineering, and math jobs within minority business enterprises;

children.label: vi
children.text: the ability to leverage resources from within the eligible entity to advance an MBDA Rural Business Center;

children.label: vii
children.text: that the mission of the eligible entity aligns with the mission of the Agency;

children.label: viii
children.text: the ability to leverage relationships with rural minority business enterprises; and

children.label: ix
children.text: a referral relationship with not less than 1 community-based organization; and

children.label: i
children.text: is located in a State or region that has a significant population of socially or economically disadvantaged individuals;

children.label: ii
children.text: has a history of serving socially or economically disadvantaged individuals; or

children.label: I
children.text: the Act of (commonly known as the “First Morrill Act”) (, chapter 130; et seq.); orJuly 2, 186212 Stat. 5037 U.S.C. 301

children.label: II
children.text: the Act of (commonly known as the “Second Morrill Act”) (, chapter 841; et seq.).August 30, 189026 Stat. 4177 U.S.C. 321

children.heading: Considerations
children.label: 3 Considerations
children.text: In determining whether to enter into an MBDA Rural Business Center agreement with an eligible entity under this section, the Under Secretary shall consider the needs of the eligible entity.
